args.message: Thanks for the pitch! A few quick questions to help me decide if this is the right fit. First, has this Tahoe been in any accidents or had major repairs? And can you confirm the service history—how recent was the last dealer service, and what's the typical maintenance been like?

text: Thanks for the pitch! A few quick questions to help me decide if this is the right fit. First, has this Tahoe been in any accidents or had major repairs? And can you confirm the service history—how recent was the last dealer service, and what's the typical maintenance been like?

speaker: seller

args.message: Great questions! Let me tell you, this Tahoe is a cream puff. No accidents to speak of, and it's been running like a top. We actually just had it in for a full dealer service about 3,000 miles ago – changed the oil, filters, the whole nine yards. These Tahoes are workhorses, and this one's no exception. Regular maintenance is key, and this one’s got a solid history. You won't be disappointed!

args.message: That's good to hear. One more thing—at 142,000 miles, have there been any transmission issues, or anything with the engine that's required more than routine maintenance? And what's the reason for selling?

text: That's good to hear. One more thing—at 142,000 miles, have there been any transmission issues, or anything with the engine that's required more than routine maintenance? And what's the reason for selling?

speaker: seller

args.message: Transmission? Engine? This Tahoe's purring like a kitten! No transmission troubles whatsoever, shifts smooth as silk. And the engine's just getting warmed up. We haven't had any major engine work pop up, just the standard stuff you'd expect from a well-maintained vehicle. As for selling? We get all sorts of great trade-ins, and this one just came through. We're just looking to move it to a happy new owner who'll appreciate its quality. Honestly, it's a fantastic deal at this price point!

speaker: seller

args.message: I hear you on the mileage, but this isn't just any Tahoe. It's a top-trim LT, and believe me, this one's been pampered. I've seen similar models, maybe even a bit rougher, go for closer to $26k at auction just last week. $22,000 is just a bit too low for what you're getting here. How about we meet in the middle at $24,000? That's a fantastic price for a vehicle of this caliber and with its documented history.

args.reason: The seller's counter at $24,000 is still $2,000 above my hard budget ceiling of $22,000. I can't stretch that far financially, and while the Tahoe sounds well-maintained, the price gap is too wide for me to close. I appreciate the details, but this doesn't work for my budget.
